text.title: Release Planning Core Rules
text.summary: Cross-team release bundling, scheduling, and communication discipline (release-train cadence, readiness matrix, deprecation comms, change-control artefacts) for paying-customer products.

rule.statement: Release cadence (weekly / biweekly / monthly) MUST be fixed and published 1 quarter ahead. Ad-hoc release dates are forbidden once the train is announced.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc dates break downstream prep; the train sets the heartbeat.

rule.statement: Each release MUST publish a readiness matrix at T-7d: engineering / QA / support / sales / marketing / legal each with status (green/yellow/red). Any red blocks the release.
rule.rationale: Without a published matrix, late-Friday surprises kill the release.

rule.statement: Breaking changes and deprecations MUST be announced >=90 days before removal. Affected customers receive direct comms plus migration tooling where feasible.
rule.rationale: <90 day deprecations damage trust and trigger contractual breach.

rule.statement: Release notes MUST be customer-facing: behaviour-change framing, not commit-log framing.
rule.rationale: Commit-log notes degrade the customer experience.

rule.statement: Each release MUST name a post-release monitor (on-call PM/eng) for T+24h with explicit rollback triggers.
rule.rationale: Hand-off ambiguity in T+24h is the most common source of slow incident response.

rule.statement: If applicability conditions fail (any Skip-If trigger holds — internal tooling; pre-PMF daily releases; one-shot launches (use launch-readiness-review); single-team product without coordination need), the agent MUST skip this methodology and route to the named alternative or to the methodology fallback.
rule.rationale: Mis-applying a methodology outside its preconditions degrades output quality and pollutes downstream artefacts; the explicit skip-rule keeps the routing tree honest.
